The Six Pillar Framework (P1-P6): - **P1: Product & Distribution (Design)**: Focus on New Product features, Target Market, and Benefit Design. [Linked Themes: Product Design, Group Products, CI, IP, LTC, PMI, State Healthcare].
- **P2: Pricing & Underwriting (Sell)**: Focus on Assumptions (Morbidity/Mortality), Rating Factors, and Selective Underwriting. [Linked Themes: Pricing, Underwriting].
- **P3: Claims Management (Pay)**: Focus on Fraud control, Triage, TPA management, and Cost containment. [Linked Themes: Claims Management].
- **P4: Risk Mgmt & Reinsurance (Protect)**: Focus on Risk Taxonomy, Reinsurance structures, and SCR/Capital. [Linked Themes: Risks, Reinsurance, Risk Management & Capital].
- **P5: Valuation & Data (Measure)**: Focus on BEL/Risk Margin, IBNR methods, Data quality, and Investments. [Linked Themes: Reserving & Valuation, Data, Models, Investments].
- **P6: Regulation & Ethics (Comply)**: Focus on IRDAI 2024 updates, PPH, and the Appointed Actuary role. [Linked Themes: Regulation, IRDAI Regulations, Professionalism].
Pricing: - **Data & Assumptions**: Experience vs Industry; internal/external credibility; proxies for new products.
- **Demographic Assumptions**: Morbidity/Mortality trends; initial vs selective selection; anti-selection risk.
- **Financial Assumptions**: Risk-free yield curves; medical inflation (~12-15% India); expense loadings (initial/renewal).
- **Risk Profile**: Impact of age, gender, occupation, and geography on claim frequency and severity.
- **Methodology**: Equation of Value (simplistic) vs. Cashflow Projection (dynamic); stochastic vs deterministic.
- **Profitability & Capital**: Target Return on Capital (RoC); Cost of Capital; risk-based capital allocation (SCR).
- **Commercial Constraints**: Competitor benchmarking; sales channel commissions; affordability vs adequacy.
- **Cross-subsidy**: Inter-generational (young subsidizing old) vs Intra-group; regulatory limits on subsidies.
- **Sensitivity & Options**: Stress testing for lapses, medical advances, and option-take up (e.g. renewal guarantees).
Claims Management: - **Notification & Proof**: Clear definitions, documentation required, verification of pre-existing conditions.
- **Assessment / Triage**: Non-disclosure checks, tele-interviews, use of specialized medical officers.
- **Control & Mitigation**: Home visits (IP), second opinions, independent medical exams.
- **Rehabilitation (IP)**: Early intervention, proportionate benefits for partial return, funding treatment to reduce claim duration.
- **Payment / Settlement**: Annuity vs lump sum, tax implications, currency, avoiding over-insurance.
Regulation: - **Key 2024 Updates**: 60-month moratorium (not 48); PED waiting period max 36 months; 1-hr/3-hr cashless TATs; 30-day Free Look; no entry age limits; Board-governed commission via PMC.
- **Key 2024 Updates**: 48-month moratorium; 1-hr/3-hr cashless timelines; 30-day Free Look; removal of entry age limits; Board-governed commission.
- **Prudential Norms**: Available Solvency Margin (ASM) >= 150% of Required Solvency Margin (RSM); statutory reserves certified by AA.
- **State Interaction**: Integration with PMJAY (Ayushman Bharat); public provision defining the floor for private demand.
- **Conduct of Business**: Treating Customers Fairly (TCF) principles; mandatory 15-day grievance resolution via GRM.
- **Capital Frameworks**: Solvency II Pillar 1/2/3 analogues; transition to Risk-Based Capital (RBC) and IFRS 17/Ind AS 117.
- **Taxation**: Section 80D benefits (up to Rs 1L for seniors); 18% GST impact on premiums and affordability.
- **Grievance Redressal**: Ombudsman hierarchy (internal -> Ombudsman -> Consumer Forum -> Civil Court).
- **Professional Roles**: Statutory role of Appointed Actuary (AA); whistleblowing; compliance with IAI APS 33/34.
IRDA Regulations: ### IRDAI (Insurance Products) Regulations 2024
- **Commencement**: In force from 1 April 2024; repeals IRDAI (Health Insurance) Regulations 2016, IRDAI (ULIP) Regulations 2019, IRDAI (Non-Linked) Regulations 2019 and others
- **Schedule III — Health**: Governs all health insurance products; general/health insurers may offer indemnity or benefit basis; life insurers may offer benefit basis only (not indemnity)

- **PED definition**: Condition diagnosed or treated within 36 months prior to policy commencement; waiting period capped at maximum 36 months continuous coverage; specific waiting period also max 36 months

- **Moratorium**: 60 continuous months of coverage (including portability and migration credits); after which no policy or claim contestable except on grounds of established fraud; enhanced sum insured has fresh 60-month clock

- **Renewability**: Insurer cannot deny renewal on grounds of prior claims; no fresh medical examination at renewal unless sum insured is being increased; grace period 15 days (monthly) / 30 days (other modes)
- **Pricing**: Premium must remain unchanged during policy term; loadings on renewal at portfolio level not individual policy level; discounts allowed for good claims experience

- **Product scope**: Insurers must offer products catering to all ages, all medical conditions including PED, chronic conditions, disabilities, HIV/AIDS, mental illness, all systems of medicine including AYUSH
### Product Management Committee (PMC)
- **Composition**: Appointed Actuary (mandatory member), Chief Marketing/Distribution Officer, CIO, CTO, Chief Compliance Officer; quorum = 3 members plus AA; CEO countersigns certifications

- **Responsibilities**: Approves all products before launch; reviews capital requirements, profitability, underwriting and reinsurance risks; approves modifications and withdrawals; monitors product performance
- **File and Use**: Prior filing to IRDAI and UIN assignment required before product launch
- **Use and File**: Insurer may launch after UIN assignment without prior IRDAI filing; PMC approval still required
- **Annual review**: Appointed Actuary reviews all products at least annually; presents findings to PMC; rate revisions must be based on credible underlying experience
### Master Circular on Health Insurance Business (May 2024)

- **Customer Information Sheet (CIS)**: Mandatory with every policy (individual and group); covers sum insured, coverage, exclusions, sub-limits, deductibles, waiting periods, free look, portability, moratorium; acknowledgement required; available in local language on request
- **Free Look Period**: 30 days from receipt of policy document for policies with term ≥ 1 year; policyholder may cancel and receive proportionate premium refund if no claim made

- **Cashless TAT**: Insurer must decide on cashless authorisation within 1 hour of request; final discharge authorisation within 3 hours; delay beyond 3 hours — additional hospital charges borne by insurer from shareholders funds

- **Claim repudiation**: No claim repudiated without PMC or 3-member Claims Review Committee (CRC) approval; reason communicated with reference to specific policy terms; documents collected by insurer/TPA — policyholder not required to submit
- **Ombudsman award**: Insurer must comply within 30 days of receipt; non-compliance attracts penalty of Rs 5,000 per day to complainant, in addition to penal interest under Ombudsman Rules 2017

- **Portability**: All credits (sum insured, NCB, waiting periods, moratorium) transferable to new insurer; existing insurer must provide data via IIB portal within 72 hours; acquiring insurer must decide within 5 days
- **Multiple policies**: For indemnity — policyholder chooses primary insurer; primary insurer coordinates with others for balance; for benefit policies — can claim from all insurers
- **NCB**: May be offered as cumulative bonus (increase in SI without premium increase) or discount on renewal premium; policyholder chooses form at renewal
- **AYUSH**: Board-approved policy required; must treat AYUSH at par with allopathy; policyholder to have choice of treatment system
- **Senior citizens**: Separate channel to be established by all insurers for health insurance claims and grievances of senior citizens
### TPA and Technology
- **TPA performance monitoring**: Board-approved criteria for monitoring TATs, service levels and customer feedback; TPA remuneration must not depend on Incurred Claim Ratio
- **ABHA number**: Ayushman Bharat Health Account — digital health record identifier; insurers may facilitate creation with specific policyholder consent; enables seamless sharing of records
- **NHCX**: National Health Claims Exchange — insurers encouraged to onboard hospitals for faster cashless settlement
- **Technology mandate**: End-to-end technology solutions required for onboarding, renewal, policy servicing, grievance redressal and claim settlement
Risks: ### SP1 Textbook: Nature of Risks (Chapters 23-24)
- **Nature of Risk Components**: Model Risk (inappropriate structure); Parameter Risk (wrong variables used); Random Fluctuations (sample error/heterogeneity).
- **Policy & Other Data Risks**: Inaccurate/incomplete records; Model Point risk (not representing portfolio); External data mismatch (e.g., using USA data for India).
- **Claim Rates (Morbidity/Mortality)**: Future parameters uncertain due to new diseases/trends; 'Expanding Funnel of Doubt' as projections go further.
- **Claim Amounts (PMI specific)**: Short-term variability in procedure costs; medical advances impact (e.g. hip replacement rehab time shifts).
- **Earlier Screening & Diagnosis**: Critical Illness risk—earlier diagnosis = earlier payment; screening prompts claims that might have otherwise lapsed.
- **Investment Performance & Assets**: Capital values risk (snapshot solvency); low reserves in CI/PMI mean low investment importance vs long-term products.
- **Expenses and Inflation**: Mismatch between inflation and loadings; volume impact (low volume = high fixed costs per policy); third-party TPA fees.
- **Persistency & Selective Withdrawal**: Selective effect (healthy leave, substandard remain); amortization risk (initial costs not recouped if early lapse).
- **Mix & Volume of New Business**: Mix by nature/size (smaller policies = lower contribution to fixed costs); Volume risk (High = capital drain; Low = overhead mismatch).
- **Guarantees and Options**: Complexity in terms offered in advance; health events harder to predict than life events; requirement for stochastic models.
- **Competition & Management Actions**: Reduced price/increased coverage to stay competitive; Management may override Actuary's prudence for market share.
- **Counterparties (RI/Distributors/TPAs)**: Reinsurer default; Distributor holding premiums; TPA commitment of expenditure on behalf of insurer.
- **Legal, Regulatory & Tax**: Retrospective changes overriding policy terms; prohibition of certain rating factors (e.g., race/genetics).
- **Reputation & Operational Fraud**: Disrepute via media/social media (especially for sick/elderly); Internal fraud / Audit failure (Embezzlement).
- **Aggregation, Concentration & Cat**: Localized outbreaks (Aggregation); industry concentration (e.g. mining industrial disease); Pandemic/War/Terrorism.
- **Non-disclosure & Anti-selection**: Moratorium approach complications; asymmetric info at proposal; genetic testing debate.
- **Product-specific: IP Insurance**: Sickness transition probabilities (inception/termination); moral hazard (return to work disincentive); leniency of definition.
- **Product-specific: CI Insurance**: Diagnosis rates; limited historical data in new markets; rider vs standalone capital requirements.
- **Product-specific: LTC Insurance**: High claim inception; transfer between dependency levels; investment/reserve significance; marketing risk (expectation of 100% cover).
- **Product-specific: PMI**: Third-party control over claims (consultants/doctors); single large claims vs accumulation; low capital strain vs long-term.
Underwriting: - **Objective**: Prevent anti-selection, classify risks equitably, maintain competitor parity.
- **Alternative Approaches**: Moratorium underwriting (often PMI), Free Cover Limits (Group).
- **Outcomes**: Standard rates, rated premiums (+%), exclusions, deferral, decline.
- **Operational**: Cost vs. Value of info, speed of onboarding (straight-through processing).
Risk Management & Capital: - **Quantification**: Stress and Scenario testing, Internal Models vs. Standard Formula.
- **Mitigation**: Reinsurance, Asset-Liability Matching (ALM), product redesign (reviewable premiums), stricter claims control.
- **Capital**: Economic capital vs. Regulatory capital, risk appetite statements, diversification benefits.
Reserving & Valuation: - **Components**: Mathematical reserves (BEL), Risk Margin, Unearned Premium Reserve (UPR), Outstanding Claims (OSLR), IBNR.
- **Methods**: Bornhuetter-Ferguson, Chain Ladder / Run-off triangles (short-tailed), Cashflow projection (long-tailed).
- **Differences**: Pricing (realistic + profit margin) vs. Supervisory (prudent/margins) vs. Embedded Value (market consistent).
- **Prudence**: Allowance for adverse deviation, discounting at risk-free rates.
Reinsurance: - **Financial**: Capital relief, profit smoothing, increase capacity.
- **Risk**: Transfer large single risks (Surplus), catastrophic events (Cat XL), aggregate shocks (Stop Loss).
- **Strategic**: 'Badging' / white-labeling, new market entry, technical underwriting assistance.
- **Types**: Proportional (Quota Share / Surplus) vs. Non-proportional (Excess of Loss).
Product Design: - **Target Market**: Segmenting by age, income, and needs; filling gaps in State provision (PMJAY etc).
- **Benefits & Conditions**: Core coverage; waiting/deferred periods; benefit limits; reinstatement terms.
- **Exclusions**: Standard medical exclusions; occupational/lifestyle risks; territorial limits.
- **Options & Guarantees**: GIOs (no medicals); conversion options; guaranteed renewability/premiums.
- **Surrender Values**: Discontinuance benefits (usually nil for indemnity PMI/CI); impact on model pricing.
- **Marketability**: Brand positioning; 'saleability' vs 'adequacy'; bundling CI/IP riders onto life cover.
- **Constraints**: IT/Admin platform capacity; reinsurance treaties; commission-driven distribution.
- **Risks**: Moral hazard (over-insurance); anti-selection at entry; complexity leading to mis-selling.
- **Profitability/Capital**: Solvency II capital strain (Pillar 1); hurdle rates; ROI vs VNB targets.
Data: - **Data Issues**: Lack of volume, heterogeneity, changes in internal processes, coding errors, lags in reporting.
- **Solutions**: Reinsurer data, Population proxies (adjusted), Grouping data.
- **Data Quality**: Accuracy, completeness, consistency, timeliness (TAS 100 / AS 34).
Models: - **Model Choice**: Multi-state (Markov) vs. Formula/Equation of Value. Deterministic vs Stochastic.
- **Governance**: Peer review, backtesting against actual experience, sensitivity testing.
- **Parameters**: Best estimate vs. Prudent, allowance for medical inflation and longevity.
Group Products: - **Structure**: Master policyholder (Employer), simplified admin, cheaper unit costs.
- **Underwriting**: Free Cover Limits (FCL), 'actively at work' condition, minimal medical info.
- **Risks**: Concentration risk (one building explodes), anti-selection if voluntary take-up, changing workforce demographics.
- **Pricing**: Experience rating (past claims) vs. Community rating, 1 or 3-year rate guarantees.
Investments: - **Nature of Liabilities**: Currency, term/duration, fixed vs real (inflation-linked).
- **Matching**: Match duration to immunize against yield curve shifts. Real assets (Equities/Property) for inflation, fixed-interest for non-linked.
- **Constraints**: Regulatory limitations on asset classes, Liquidity needs (PMI claims are sudden), Solvency II capital charges on risky assets.
Professional Guidance: - **Standards**: Following APS/TAS (e.g. TAS 100), ensuring advice is clear, comprehensive, and unbiased.
- **Role**: Statutory duties (Chief Actuary / With-Profits Actuary), Peer review requirements.
- **Ethics**: Whistleblowing, managing conflicts of interest, handling data quality issues responsibly.
Capital & Solvency: - **Role of Capital**: Provide confidence to policyholders and regulators, buffer against shocks.
- **Solvency II**: Pillar 1 (Quantitative: BEL, Risk Margin, SCR), Pillar 2 (Qualitative: ORSA, Governance), Pillar 3 (Disclosure).
- **Management**: Optimizing capital via Reinsurance or ALM, Return on Capital (ROC) thresholds.
Private Medical Insurance (PMI): - **Nature**: Short-term (annual renewable), high frequency/low severity claims.
- **Pricing**: Community rating, Age-banding, Medical inflation is a massive driver (super-inflation).
- **Claims**: Pre-authorization, Provider networks, Excesses/Co-payments to reduce trivial claims.
- **Underwriting**: Moratorium is very common, FMU (Full Medical) for comprehensive cover.
Critical Illness: - **Nature**: Long-term, fixed lump-sum payment upon diagnosis of defined condition.
- **Risks**: Changes in medical science (earlier diagnosis), anti-selection, definition disputes.
- **Features**: Survival period (usually 14-28 days), standalone vs accelerated (attached to life cover), severity-based payouts.
Income Protection: - **Nature**: Replacement of lost earnings due to incapacity. Regular payments.
- **Key Levers**: Deferred period (DP) length, Replacement Ratio (max 50-75% to maintain incentive to work).
- **Definition**: Own occupation vs Any occupation vs Activities of Daily Work.
- **Risks**: Moral hazard, economic downturns heavily increase claim rates ('unemployment disguise').
Pricing & Rating: - **Age/Gender**: Classic variables (though gender restricted in EU).
- **Experience Rating**: Adjusting renewal premiums based on past group claims.
- **NCD**: No Claims Discounts (common in PMI) to prevent trivial claims but hurts retention if lost.
- **Reviewability**: Guaranteed (fixed) vs Reviewable (can hike rates if experience worsens over the block).
State Healthcare & Demographics: - **State Role**: Universal coverage (PMJAY / NHS), means-tested social care.
- **Private Role**: Que-jumping, luxury facilities, covering non-critical procedures.
- **Demographics**: Aging populations increase LTC and medical costs, shrinking taxpayer bases.
- **Healthcare Economics**: QALYs (Quality Adjusted Life Years) to decide drug funding via agencies like NICE.
Long-Term Care (LTC): - **Nature**: Payout triggered by failure of Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) or cognitive impairment.
- **Types**: Pre-funded (regular premiums while healthy) vs Immediate Needs Annuity (lump sum at point of care).
- **Risks**: Extreme longevity risk, massive care-cost inflation, reputational risk if claims denied in old age.
Legislation & Taxation: - **Premiums/Benefits**: Are premiums tax deductible? Are benefits tax free? Drives product demand.
- **Employer Tax**: Incentivizes Group PMI / Group IP if treated favorably as business expense.
- **Anti-Discrimination**: Equality acts preventing pricing based on genetics, gender, or disabilities without pure statistical proof.
Product Design (Protection): - **Standalone vs Riders**: Selling CI or Death cover attached to a savings/mortgage plan.
- **Unit-Linked**: Charging mortality/morbidity costs by deducting units from a fund (reviewable by nature).
- **Options**: Conversion options, Guaranteed Insurability Options (GIOs) without medicals.
Administration: - **Setup Constraints**: Can legacy IT handle complex tiered benefits or unit-deductions?
- **Customer Journey**: Quotation portals, premium collection methods (Direct Debit).
- **Outsourcing**: Using Third Party Administrators (TPAs) for claims, call centers. Retaining oversight.
